zh.lifeSummary: MARGARET DE CLARE是Robert Stonard的女兒，於1365年出生在英國可瓦爾特魯羅。她和METCALFE KAYLE至少有1個女兒。她去世於1385年在她的家鄉享年20歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: MARGARET DE CLARE是Robert Stonard的女儿，于1365年出生在英国可瓦爾特魯羅。她和METCALFE KAYLE至少有1个女儿。她去世于1385年在她的家乡享年20岁。

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: CLARE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: MARGARET
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: MARGARET DE CLARE was born in 1365, in Truro, Cornwall, England as the daughter of Robert Stonard. She had at least 1 daughter with METCALFE KAYLE. She died in 1385, in her hometown, at the age of 20.

fr.lifeSummary: MARGARET DE CLARE est née en 1365, à Truro, Cornouailles, Angleterre, fille de Robert Stonard. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec METCALFE KAYLE. Elle est décédée en 1385, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 20 ans.

de.lifeSummary: MARGARET DE CLARE wurde 1365 in Truro, Cornwall, England, als Tochter von Robert Stonard geboren. Sie hatte mit METCALFE KAYLE mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1385 im Alter von 20 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: MARGARET DE CLARE è nata il nel 1365 nel Truro, Cornwall, England come figlia di Robert Stonard. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con METCALFE KAYLE. Morì nel 1385, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 20 anni.

ja.Truro, Cornwall, England: イギリスコルンワルトルロ
ja.lifeSummary: MARGARET DE CLAREは1365年にイギリスコルンワルトルロでRobert Stonardの娘として生まれた。彼女はMETCALFE KAYLEとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1385年に故郷で20歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: MARGARET DE CLARE(은)는 1365년 영국 코른월 트루로에서 Robert Stonard의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 METCALFE KAYLE(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1385년 그녀의 고향에서 20세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: MARGARET DE CLARE nasceu em 1365, em Truro, Cornwall, Inglaterra filha de Robert Stonard. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com METCALFE KAYLE. Ela faleceu em 1385, em sua cidade natal, com 20 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: MARGARET DE CLARE родилась в 1365 г. в местности труро, корнвалл, Англия. Её отца звали Robert Stonard. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от METCALFE KAYLE. Она умерла в 1385 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 20 лет.

es.lifeSummary: MARGARET DE CLARE nació en 1365, en Truro, Cornwall, Inglaterra como hija de Robert Stonard. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con METCALFE KAYLE. Murió en 1385, en su pueblo, a la edad de 20 años.
